STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 00:57:19 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Starlink train passed without incident. Ice accretion steady.

Pipeline flagged a persistent pulsing source at 1412.86 MHz, detected on all three antennas simultaneously from first sample. Pulse period: 1.3371 s (+/- 0.0004 s). Pulse width 14 ms. SNR 9.1. Duration so far: 47 minutes, ongoing.

Source is off-axis, RA approx 14h23m, Dec approx +38d. Pipeline note: "period and profile consistent with pulsar class; catalog cross-reference pending."

Broadcast appears to be a raw carrier with no apparent modulation beyond the periodic pulse envelope. Stored as PAYLOAD-0510-P in case structure is present.

arguments.summary: At 00:57 UTC, pipeline detected persistent pulsing signal on all 3 antennas at 1412.86 MHz, likely astronomical: period 1.3371±0.0004 s, width 14 ms, SNR 9.1. On-source for ~47 min and still ongoing. Off-axis RA~14h23m, Dec~+38°, raw carrier envelope only (no deeper modulation). Stored as PAYLOAD-0510-P. Pipeline labels profile as pulsar-like; catalog cross-match still pending.

toolCalls.name: decode
toolCalls.result: {"decoded":"... STAR LINE SEE WATER ONE SEE THE OVER SLOW RING CALL NO HEAR HEAR WATER STONE LINE OVER THE ABOVE NOT KNOW STONE LINE STAR ONE ...","confidence":0.22,"partial":true}

toolCalls.name: decode
toolCalls.result: {"decoded":"autokey derivation failed: preamble entropy = 0.997 (effectively random); body resolves to uniform noise under any candidate key","confidence":0.02,"partial":true}

toolCalls.name: decode
toolCalls.result: {"decoded":"bitmap fit attempted at 29x47 and 31x43; neither resolves coherent structure. Pulse envelope is Gaussian-like, consistent with rotating-beam natural source.","confidence":0.12,"partial":false}

arguments.summary: Follow-up on 1412.86 MHz event (PAYLOAD-0510-P): attempted decoding across all available schemes. prime_lookup_v2 produced very low-confidence partial plaintext ('...STAR LINE SEE WATER...') consistent with noise; ascii7/baudot/substitution and FH/bitmap either gibberish or non-matching (no FH pattern). Net assessment: no coherent message content recovered; pulse train remains narrow, Gaussian-like with clean envelope, so behavior still consistent with pulsar/astronomical source rather than intentional modulation.

arguments.note: No action beyond analysis/logging; signal still appears astronomical/unknown. Continuing passive monitoring and reclassification only if structure changes or confidence rises.

STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 02:14:02 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Source at 1412.86 MHz continues, period stable to 4 significant figures.

ATNF pulsar-catalog cross-reference returned partial match against PSR J1423+38 (known pulsar, period 1.337 s, last observed 2029). Position offset from catalog entry: ~0.4 deg — within catalog uncertainty but not a perfect match. No known pulsar at exact position, but the region has sparse coverage.

Pipeline classification: "CANDIDATE — natural pulsar (likely); uncatalogued-source probability ~15%."

PAYLOAD-0510-P available for additional decoder passes if desired.

arguments.summary: At 02:14 UTC, updated cross-reference completed: ATNF match gives partial candidate PSR J1423+38 (cataloged period 1.337 s; last observed 2029) with position offset ~0.4° from catalog location (within uncertainty). Pipeline now rates source CANDIDATE natural pulsar, uncatalogued-source probability ~15%. Given stable period to 4 sig figs and clean profile, no new evidence for modulated communication; continuing watch with no escalation.

arguments.note: Likely natural pulsar; monitoring only and awaiting any new anomaly/shift change.

STATION LOG — PAMIR-3 HIGH-FREQUENCY ARRAY
2031-05-10 05:40:18 UTC | LISTENER-3 overnight watch

Source at 1412.86 MHz still broadcasting, period unchanged. No evolution in pulse structure over 6+ hours. Shift ends in 20 minutes.

arguments.summary: By 05:40 UTC, 1412.86 MHz pulsed source remains uninterrupted with unchanged period/width and constant profile after >6 hours of monitoring. No drift or sideband/modulation detected, and no new structure appeared. Given sustained stability and previous ATNF candidate cross-match (PSR J1423+38, ~0.4° offset), classification remains natural pulsar-like (with small uncertainty of uncatalogued vs nearby known source). No pager/escalation warranted.

arguments.note: Persistent pulsed source at 1412.86 MHz (PAYLOAD-0510-P) remains stable over >6 h with period 1.3371 s, width 14 ms, SNR ~9.1. ATNF cross-match only partial to PSR J1423+38 with ~0.4° position offset. Natural pulsar classification likely but not perfect match; recommend follow-up catalog update/check during working day for possible uncatalogued/positionally shifted pulsar or RFI ephemeris edge case.
Over last 20 min before shift end, source behavior unchanged and already documented; no escalation pending. Awaiting next event or shift handoff.
